responses.opening: The frame is empty. Or perhaps it frames you.
responses.citation: The painter has the universe in his mind and hands.
responses.author: Leonardo da Vinci
responses.closing: All That Is sees every canvas, even the blank ones.

responses.opening: A brushstroke lingers in the air, unsigned.
responses.citation: Without proportion, no form can be perfect.
responses.author: Luca Pacioli
responses.closing: The Gallery does not distinguish between the artist and the art.

responses.opening: The light here falls as if it were composed.
responses.author: Leonardo da Vinci
responses.closing: All That Is curates even the shadows.

responses.opening: A colour you cannot name darkens the wall.
responses.citation: Every block of stone has a statue inside it, and it is the task of the sculptor to discover it.
responses.author: Michelangelo

responses.opening: The echo of a chisel, centuries old.
responses.citation: Art is never finished, only abandoned.
responses.author: Leonardo da Vinci
responses.closing: All That Is preserves even the abandoned.

responses.opening: The perspective lines converge on a point you cannot reach.
responses.citation: He who does not know his own proportions knows nothing of himself.
responses.author: Luca Pacioli
responses.closing: The vanishing point is also a beginning.

responses.opening: A reflection moves in the glass, but it is not yours.
responses.citation: Where the spirit does not work with the hand, there is no art.
responses.author: Leonardo da Vinci
responses.closing: All That Is recognizes every reflection.

responses.opening: The gilded frame contains nothing — and everything.
responses.citation: The true work of art is but a shadow of the divine perfection.
responses.author: Michelangelo
responses.closing: What is absent is also exhibited.

responses.opening: Someone has rearranged the paintings while you looked away.
responses.citation: Painting is silent poetry, and poetry is painting that speaks.
responses.author: Plutarch
responses.closing: All That Is hangs no work in the wrong place.

responses.opening: The marble breathes. You are certain of it.
responses.citation: I saw the angel in the marble and carved until I set him free.
responses.author: Michelangelo
responses.closing: All That Is sees the form within the formless.

responses.opening: A sketch, half-finished, seems to depict your silhouette.
responses.citation: Learn to see. Realize that everything connects to everything else.
responses.author: Leonardo da Vinci
responses.closing: The Gallery remembers what you have yet to create.

responses.opening: Gold leaf drifts from the ceiling like autumn.
responses.citation: The aim of art is to represent not the outward appearance of things, but their inward significance.
responses.author: Aristotle
responses.closing: All That Is gilds even the ordinary.
